name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: An update on the pandemic from the U.S. Senate:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e9a2/6cd27893/538c6808/1590322f/3593921207/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | These are anxious, unsettling times, and I want to check in and share some thoughts about the coronavirus pandemic. This is a public health crisis. It's an economic crisis. And it's affecting every single family in this country. But here's what I know:**we're going to get through this.** I am pushing each day for the federal and state governments to be aggressive with solutions. **First, we need to meet the urgent health care needs.** Ramp up testing, meet the urgent needs for more masks and gowns, produce more ventilators, prepare our hospitals for a surge. **We also have to help families and small businesses bridge the cataclysmic economic impacts of shuttering huge swaths of our society****.** We need a massive program to bridge the abyss, but we can't let corporate lobbyists win huge no-strings-attached bail outs. That means people-centered policies, like building on the new paid leave plan that just passed Congress. I'm also pushing for tens of billions of dollars to help keep renters in their homes. Elizabeth Warren and I successfully argued for a national moratorium on evictions and foreclosures. We need help for small businesses who have to close their doors. And if we do need taxpayer help for some industries to keep the lights on, we need to put workers ahead of shareholders, and insist on real guard rails like making companies that benefit maintain their payrolls, offer paid leave, allow union organizing, and end stock buybacks forever. Another of my goals is to make sure that Americans who were already struggling to get by don't fall through the cracks while we work to address this pandemic. [**If you're able, please make a donation to Feeding America, so families won't go hungry**** _right now_****.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e9a2/6cd27893/538c6808/1590322c/3593921207/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-feedingamerica?refcode=2020-03-18-covid&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") To get through this, we need to stay diligent and listen to our public health and scientific communities: stay home as much as you can; wash your hands and clean surfaces regularly; avoid close interactions with others outside your home; and cover your coughs and sneezes. I'm going to keep you up-to-date as we go. [**Stay tuned and, if you're able, please make a donation to Feeding America to support vulnerable members of our community.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e9a2/6cd27893/538c6808/1590322c/3593921207/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-feedingamerica?refcode=2020-03-18-covid&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100")**[](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e9a2/6cd27893/538c6808/1590322d/3593921207/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-orfoodbank?refcode=2020-03-18-covid-or&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100")** **_Jeff_** [Donate to Feeding America](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e9a2/6cd27893/538c6808/1590322c/3593921207/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-feedingamerica?refcode=2020-03-18-covid&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") |

subject: Coronavirus Relief
body:
Assistance on the way... No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-v/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Coronavirus Relief](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-j/)
The coronavirus pandemic is having a major impact on the everyday lives of Americans from coast to coast. In this week's blog I thought I'd update you on the coronavirus relief legislation passed by the House over the weekend. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [Coronavirus](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-d/)
Coronavirus (COVID-19) is on everyone's mind. In fact, the first three cases of Coronavirus have been confirmed in Ohio. In this week's blog I discuss this ongoing challenge, what steps have been taken from the executive and legislative level, and finally offer some words of hope and encouragement. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [My Take on Super Tuesday](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-u/)
Yesterday was Super Tuesday. 14 states across the country held their primaries, and one territory, American Samoa. The results were pretty interesting. Joe Biden, who looked like his campaign was on life-support, won BIG. And the press dutifully changed their tone. Joe is now the comeback kid! [Read more >](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-o/) [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [Bernie and Fidel](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhdtkhjy-kujkulhluu-p/)
In the course of his political career, the presumptive Democratic nominee for the presidency, Bernie Sanders has had many wacky points of view. In this week's blog, I'll focus on one - Bernie's admiration of the communist dictator, Fidel Castro. name: Mike Siegel
subject: More needs to be done
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE SIEGEL FOR CONGRESS](https://click
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16262655/172564672/683764388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYzc4MGRmYS1lODY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tAoRNmPZ_T7Xi7Kka1IkF2G-gmh9f34mc1wA2vr_BLM=&emci=193312f2-e869-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=dc780dfa-e869-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6233172)
---
Peter,

**This is the moment when our federal government must step up: to give relief to impacted Americans; and to build the solutions that will resolve this crisis.**

The first need is immediate; we need direct economic aid. Countless people have already lost work, lost business, and lost control over their financial situations. We live in a country where 40% of us cannot afford $400 in an emergency. The tremendous inequality of wealth has made us even more vulnerable to a crisis like this one.

**For restaurant workers and construction workers, for drivers and musicians — for so many of us, immediate cash payments from the U.S. Treasury will provide an essential lifeline.**

But that will not be enough. We have a broken healthcare system that is unequipped to handle this crisis. Rural hospitals have been closing. We lack essential supplies like testing kits and critical equipment like ventilators. We cannot rely on international supply chains as borders are closing and our trading partners have their own crises to address.

**We must act now to build the solutions we need.** After calls from citizens and lawmakers, Donald Trump invoked the Defense Production Act — a law that allows the government to take control of the crisis and begin producing the equipment that our country so desperately needs. Unfortunately, he has not taken any concrete steps using this authority.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16262656/172564673/203122784?refcode=em031920&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYzc4MGRmYS1lODY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tAoRNmPZ_T7Xi7Kka1IkF2G-gmh9f34mc1wA2vr_BLM=)
Under President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, when the time came to enter World War II and fight fascism abroad, we took decisive action. FDR convened the captains of industry and innovation to address the need for war materials. He used the Treasury to guide investment: to build facilities where U.S. manufacturers could immediately begin the construction of tanks, jets, ships, and more.

**We need a similar, immediate, and massive show of leadership from the Executive Branch today. We also need the Legislative Branch to step up as a co-equal branch of government with the power of the purse.**

Some positive steps have been taken, but we need to continue pushing for smart economic policy. And we need to establish a commitment: that as a nation we will use our resources to protect the lives of the people.

**Let’s work to come out of this crisis stronger than ever. Let’s come out of this crisis together.**

Fighting for you,
Mike Siegel

name: Josh Hicks
subject: What this crisis is showing us
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
Friends,

What we’re going through as Kentuckians — as a nation — was inconceivable just days ago. And more than anything, COVID-19 is showing the need to protect our most vulnerable populations.

Our healthcare system was in crisis before the pandemic.

Now, more than ever, we have to be concerned about the quality, quantity, and viability of our rural hospitals. And people should be able to go to the doctor without fear of medical bankruptcy.

Whether someone is elderly or infirmed, lives in a depressed area where the economic outlook isn’t good, or has suffered job-related injuries like black lung — our friends and neighbors need our support right now.

Our economy was in crisis before the pandemic.

One of the most distressing things to come out of this is the headlong rush to protect stock market gains instead of focusing on protecting people’s lives. That’s a failing of our government — and it’s shared across the parties.

Congress’ first action should be to protect lives, increase ventilator output and supplies, and bail out people — not corporations.

Without a healthy population, the stock market gains don’t do anybody any good unless they’re at the top of the pyramid.

There’s a way out of this crisis. Together.

For now, it means social distancing, taking safety precautions, and making sure your neighbors are taken care of. When we’re on the other side of this, it means building real solutions with regular folks — not corporations — at the center of those solutions.

Yesterday, my team sent along three ways you can support Kentuckians right now — and I wanted to make sure you saw them again.

Read the email below — if you need help, fill out this survey from Mutual Aid Lexington: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeP4koOM1yO-tb6VicTWjZhCHTSrarceghikJROf6QL2Nc8eQ/viewform?fbclid=IwAR3DIeUtcpBhW3fFEPHZ2rtgaK4ewlzAiiBDOHvfByJ5rHzKyWzuD7Fb7UM. If you can give your time or a little bit of money right now, please read the options below and find out where you can best contribute.

Thank you,

Josh

---------- Forwarded message ---------

From: Team Hicks

Date: Tues, Mar 17, 2020 at 3:56 PM

Subject: How you can support your fellow Kentuckians right now

Friends,

We should all be very concerned about our neighbors, our families, and ourselves right now.

Right now, we all have to pull together — we’ve got to make some tough decisions personally to keep our whole community safe. This is the time where we all have to pull together to try to flatten the curve so that our hospitals don’t get overwhelmed.

We are taking all precautionary measures as suggested by Governor Andy Beshear, and that means we have postponed all upcoming public events. All of us have a duty to be part of the solution and not part of the problem.

So, today, we want to ask you to support local organizations who are working to provide vital resources to our most vulnerable populations:

God’s Pantry Food Bank is a critical front-line resource for our neighbors in need. A $25 donation helps them buy 250 pounds of food and deliver it to 400 food pantries and meal programs they support in Central and Eastern Kentucky. You can donate to God’s Pantry by clicking here:
https://godspantry.org/donate/covid19/

Blue Grass Community Foundation funds organizations and programs that have deep roots in Kentucky and strong experience working to provide residents with access to food, prescriptions and healthcare, and other childcare and basic needs. Your donation to Bluegrass Community Foundation will help those in economically depressed areas hit hardest by loss of work or reduced wages, and school and business closures. You can contribute to Blue Grass Community Foundation here:
https://bgcf.givingfuel.com/coronavirus

Mutual Aid Lexington is building a network of folks to support their neighbors at this time. They’re helping coordinate everything from meals and transportation to childcare, eldercare, and pet care. Fill out this form here [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeP4koOM1yO-tb6VicTWjZhCHTSrarceghikJROf6QL2Nc8eQ/viewform?fbclid=IwAR3DIeUtcpBhW3fFEPHZ2rtgaK4ewlzAiiBDOHvfByJ5rHzKyWzuD7Fb7UM] to let coordinators know what help you need and/or what you can give. And if you’re not sure how to help, fill out the form and they’ll connect you with a way to get involved.

Last, but not least, if you or someone in your family is experiencing symptoms, please visit this website set up by Kentucky Health and Family Services or call Kentucky’s COVID-19 hotline at 800-722-5725.
https://chfs.ky.gov/agencies/dph/pages/covid19.aspx

From day one, this campaign has been about what’s best for everyday Kentuckians. And though our mission is different in this moment — it’s more important than ever.

Thank you for being a part of this team.

-- Team Hicks

name: Dean Browning
subject: COVID-19
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Dear Patriot,
The COVID-19, also known as coronavirus, has become the singular focus for America and the world. With that comes the question, are we overreacting? My response to that is to quote from an article by Tom Bossert where he said, "The paradox of the coronavirus response is that it only works if we take aggressive steps before they seem necessary and, if they work, will not have seemed necessary. Success will look like we overreacted. This is a unique emergency, but the basics still apply."
[CLICK HERE TO READ THE FULL ARTICLE!](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KTOlkUScQzD_bkOgKuYckesNyD5uaAtbR4cgc_2Q5hHhx59ITGj9l5hZRHhGJ8JsiTj9Dt-2H0a23JvBAPMmGS3a0EQBjj56VlN8yjc0v1uBZVDfhs0HTWTNwHxQiOTS5uLEmYdZxzb49obVhZ4JjJZpKQHlGOwD2qSCgVuCg3n7Rx3gqvC4G1CpbAdLmxgSsm9rMqfzZz6n-SIQlipsn3asBhLgeTRICgNRo58wGSdVbbp0Gk70d76uRyxYYLS4&c=E3y8gOFpaYY1bS0OYmrM77P64Z_1F0InHl747NMgq2aWdv7cDv848Q==&ch=aaZHFS4iVuFOfBSFzyf3H_F0nfFlY2BNfnRocPexexPs1KukxbxqeQ==) We all need to do our part in helping to prevent the spread of the virus. Please keep updated on COVID-19 by visiting the Center for Disease Control's website: [www.cgc.gov](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KTOlkUScQzD_bkOgKuYckesNyD5uaAtbR4cgc_2Q5hHhx59ITGj9l5hZRHhGJ8Jsk6uKWZilCSx-T58oRgMAj1pWCCIphBJOVkmp2AIBjRwbVNTCtYl8c0mvUeiIl2gUJtsUH7yKT28=&c=E3y8gOFpaYY1bS0OYmrM77P64Z_1F0InHl747NMgq2aWdv7cDv848Q==&ch=aaZHFS4iVuFOfBSFzyf3H_F0nfFlY2BNfnRocPexexPs1KukxbxqeQ==). Also, keep in mind the following common-sense items to protect yourself and your family.

* Know the symptoms of the virus, which include shortness of breath, cough, and fever. If you think you are experiencing symptoms, please contact your doctor.
* Please wash your hands. Wash your hands for at least 20 seconds. If there is not a sink near you, then use a hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol until you can get to a place with soap and water.
* Stay home if you do not feel well; going out could worsen the spread of the virus and expose at-risk groups like senior citizens.
* Cover your mouth or nose with your elbow or a tissue when you cough or sneeze.

Thank you for actively doing your part to stop the spread of the COVID-19. Americans are resilient and resourceful, so there is no doubt we will get through this crisis. Please be safe and take care of yourself and your family. God Bless,
---
|
---
| Dean Browning Republican for Congress, PA-07
P.S. - While this virus has affected many aspects our campaign, I am confident we have the tools and grassroots support to overcome this virus and take back the House in November!
---

Team, We're faced with an unprecedented health crisis and we're using our campaign platform to get the word out about staying safe and following the [CDC's recommendations for social distancing](https://act.myngp.com/el/UOaMmBGF9oLgpUpY219m4QAtf_tFYmj3TqTBemgBqEc=/047JhVWVlG2Q7wSHXB6MXUv6aEhFnfKHGl4X8vCbr64=) by canceling all in-person events and moving our organizing efforts online. This is especially important in our district which is one of the most diverse in the country, with many multi-generational families living under the same roofs. Please be mindful in practicing social distancing, especially with your family elders. We're monitoring things on a daily basis, and will be sure to share any critical updates with you. We also remain committed to the work we have ahead of us to win in November, including raising the resources to continue getting the word out about the important issues at stake in this race: affordable healthcare, lowering prescription drug prices, and protecting Medicaid and Social Security. Governor Holcomb and the Indiana State Department of Health announced some BIG news – they’re partnering with Indiana-based Eli Lilly and Company to | | |
---|---|---
Friend,

Governor Holcomb and the Indiana State Department of Health announced some BIG news – they’re partnering with Indiana-based Eli Lilly and Company to accelerate coronavirus testing across Indiana!

**Lilly will use its specialized research labs to conduct COVID-19 tests, expanding testing ability in the state so that more Hoosiers can receive a timely diagnosis.**

_**As Governor Holcomb said, “This partnership will be transformational in our efforts to accelerate testing for COVID-19. We are grateful for Lilly’s dedication to the health and safety of Hoosiers as we continue to put all of our focus into slowing the spread.”**_

But that’s not all. Here are a few more of the latest Indiana developments as we all work together to slow the spread of the coronavirus:

* All Indiana **public schools are currently closed** – whether they’re using e-learning, on spring break, or are using waiver days granted by Governor Holcomb.
* Small businesses are the backbone of our state. That’s why the U.S. Small Business Administration has issued [an Economic Injury Disaster Loan declaration,](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=bfbaed177c4a49d089c60911b8140aad&_e=FxJT2ApwV7fdXb-5w7F9W0gFDz97nJe2NkuM_VbGooeFS8iWyILQHtId2ekYXRKc9vhaKczGwnm6c7ZPir6Cisi8uusC0fRJTh7T85zWEI0b0YWPEKwFjaUHwlzVgJZDgVGEP3qIv22kstbaYMalMiqg9owKwcLf-FnPjS6F-drv4tNYootA_Q3pf6uKb-ek6yWThO4lccKg3wXqOHS_fYoCjURwCetwuQyQPuqWd5Qz_mH9onicuDHGFMYeHIKOIVvd5WY-zFaI2_KU1cdTCjvPV_VxTHFOG8uMMo93Sxrz-Olup2qSXWzKoZFrVEP8) per Governor Holcomb's request, which would provide **targeted, low-interest loans to Indiana small businesses** to help them overcome temporary revenue loss.
* Looking to apply for unemployment insurance benefits? The Department of Workforce Development has **two upcoming webinars** to learn about eligibility, FAQs, and how to apply. [Learn more here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=bfbaed177c4a49d089c60911b8140aad&_e=FxJT2ApwV7fdXb-5w7F9W4O7RVfBxqM0fi3tFAkSWSx-BRIkQ0yG_9fJ8FfQhlmTaMF1yVk7-9TL5gtgAP2mJ88hCMffSUt2ADCx2_hkz4xQHsvmWTqgokownEDTklbhvCzMVTXN5EQMoJuZqkFKfUQXw-NXWeT_0QCyBPc2cYoVZ-FdwPaWCl1BnnCMtNtEknekCNlHs74aN_D9QF-b1YIL8xuZm4vel2VcWLnSWqBH5vENNTTL7fodx8Codhz6L76XdBgCIeXiSroCGSj-P-4ufOgoZDM-gddqd33Zaj8Sk3PCuQMSsxMDkYe30ghDGLBxaKqmN2P8dhJV3gIut34VOXSD3jXMrldLBlZigrxhePRFzrfIjtOimpi5Qzib7Xez_uJFECHTE8-4fqU9wQ%3D%3D)
* The Department of Revenue is still **ready to provide great customer service** at 317-232-2240 or [online here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=bfbaed177c4a49d089c60911b8140aad&_e=FxJT2ApwV7fdXb-5w7F9WzoPBYqOR8cTDWSV5cWTpc8EBojv3JJ-aq3RN9UlM1TR6MJjkKqKDnIq2s7qlPxVTLSVMuGYzGHKAgQPU9htW8rqTBmSP8Ju3XX04RccMQVPYaLv51X2m_7TjRAAQ1x1d2O6xx8zwXB74IigfiPn4iLKgTrM60lYqIMUD2WD4kag_ZOXlooRRS9w4miEE--FvknTmfJCeIKBAY81uD_v2Cn1N-ls5SuYe91uWBqj-PglAwDYnIM6Zh4j5s3iQyAGjw%3D%3D)
* If you have questions about the coronavirus, you can **call the Indiana State Department of Health** toll-free 24/7 at 877-826-0011 or visit their [website here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=bfbaed177c4a49d089c60911b8140aad&_e=FxJT2ApwV7fdXb-5w7F9W1YdwRehR3UCNzt7m6IzzQ78EJ_0U3YMQR3__fUj7sUHwb-BzXeVSiLVFd09z6Z955GSyXyEfuqOmfem6U1SpMF9CavSg2ywPgAcVUr5bBFXWL4-PjtJNASlNUKqG_RyMH_1019dr2cqIgjyfoFS3OL_TkNHDo6YDrjb9lpBBB-hF0Crr49lu-fj9qum1cqJJ0TU4GnKGgfQe8J-2pW9MwIEkz9ki6YPpS9wC_xq6S-0MGbb-98tydIe90ipn3kvpw%3D%3D)

We’re all in this together – and thanks to this innovative Indiana-based company, more testing resources are on the way!

We’ll continue to keep sending you the latest news on the actions Governor Holcomb is taking with top medical leaders to keep Hoosiers safe.

Thanks,
-Holcomb Crew

subject: Sen. Cornyn's words vs. his actions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e25e/514690930/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter:

After nearly two decades in Washington, one thing is plain to see -- you can trust Senator John Cornyn to side with the pharmaceutical companies and the insurance industry over his constituents.

You have my word: I will always side with Texans. **That 's why I'm running against Sen. Cornyn.** In the Senate, I'll protect our health care and push for a public option so anyone who wants to can buy into Medicare.

**Will you stand with me in this fight for our health care by contributing today?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $5>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e25d/514690930/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=5")**
**[Express donate $10>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e25c/514690930/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e253/514690930/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e252/514690930/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=50")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e251/514690930/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__")**
**While Senator Cornyn spends a lot of time _talking_ about wanting to reduce the cost of prescription drugs, his _actions_ paint a completely different picture.** He took hundreds of thousands of dollars from Big Pharma and then immediately turned around and voted against a bipartisan bill that would have lowered the cost of prescription drugs.

Senator Cornyn has worked hand-in-hand with Mitch McConnell to:
1. Gut protections for _millions_ of Texans with pre-existing conditions
2. Allow insurance companies to charge older Texans _more_ for coverage

**[If you think it's time that Texans have a senator who will defend their health care and work to improve it (not tear it down), donate to my campaign today.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e250/514690930/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Senator Cornyn is just another politician in the pockets of corporate PACs. He's forgotten who he was elected to serve: the people of Texas.

I know Texans aren't afraid to fight for what we believe in. I don't believe in backing down from tough fights -- but I need you on this one, Peter.

**[Join my team today by making a quick donation. Help me defend our health care.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e250/514690930/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Thanks,
MJ

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890217/7f51f7bc/4c1951f9/4ef1e250/514690930/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.3nd__1088__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Trump Signs Coronavirus Aid Package with Paid Sick Leave, Free Testing
body:
"President Trump on Wednesday signed into law a multibillion-dollar emergency aid package aimed at helping Americans impacted by the coronavirus." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ce828f90f3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Trump Signs Coronavirus Aid Package with Paid Sick Leave, Free Testing**

“President Trump on Wednesday signed into law a multibillion-dollar emergency aid package aimed at helping Americans impacted by the coronavirus,” Morgan Chalfant reports in _The Hill_.

The bipartisan measure, resulting from negotiations last week between the Trump Administration and Congress, “includes provisions offering paid leave benefits for Americans, bolstered unemployment benefits and free diagnostic testing for the virus.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f54456f079&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Americans know how to rally against an enemy. Our nation was established on the principle that nothing can break us; no force is strong enough to withstand our undying fervor to preserve freedom and enjoy the lives that we choose to lead. Once again, Americans are coming together to defend the nation against a common threat,” Charlotte Pence Bond writes in [_The Washington Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4ed8741d10&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “In what can only be described as a moment of unconscious self-parody, the media pretended not to understand why Trump would refer to the virus in geographic terms—as if they’d never heard of the Spanish Flu, West Nile Virus, Ebola, Zika, Lyme Disease, or the many other diseases named for their places of origin. As if they haven’t been doing the same thing for months,” John Daniel Davidson writes in [_The Federalist_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=43d4849022&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “Through a series of lies, blunders and cover-ups, the communist government of China has intensified the spread of the novel coronavirus . . . After decades of unethical trade practices and human rights violations, the coronavirus crisis should be the final straw against China's case for global leadership,” Rep. Michael Cloud (R-TX) writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=76388bc370&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=54b9bc461d&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b4cd04a721&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=3f5ec227ed)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Will=20Douglas?=
subject: COVID-19 and my campaign
body:
After much thought, and in an abundance of caution, we have decided to temporarily postpone all public events hosted by my campaign. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/willdouglasfortexas/covid-19-eblast-march-19-2020?e=4f010155df)
---
| | [ ](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=d4999efbfb&e=4f010155df)
---
| | Friends,

After much thought, and in an abundance of caution, we have decided to temporarily postpone all public events hosted by my campaign. This will include all scheduled house parties, fundraisers, and block walks. To all of those who have already RSVP'd for these events, we will let you know once they have been rescheduled.

In addition to the campaign, I also recently announced that my pharmacies will be temporarily closing our walk-in business to protect the health of both our employees and customers. We will still be servicing via our drive-thru and curbside service.

I think it is important that we take the advice of Governor Abbott and remain particularly vigilant about the potential spread of COVID-19. Please direct yourself to the [state's website here](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=568ea6ee06&e=4f010155df) with any questions about health and prevention.

While we are currently postponing public events, that does not mean that our campaign will be taking one minute off! We will be taking full advantage of social media platforms and other technology to to keep our supporters connected and get our message out. If you have not already done so, please follow me on [Facebook](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=3e22a99cff&e=4f010155df), [Twitter](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=e32dbcd242&e=4f010155df), and [Instagram](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=aef493d5f0&e=4f010155df).

I also would encourage anyone that would like to get in touch with me and discuss the campaign to message me on social media or email me at [willdouglastx@gmail.com](mailto:willdouglastx@gmail.com).

I hope that you all remain safe, and watch out for friends and neighbors in need.

God Bless!

\- Will
---
|
---

clean_body: After much thought, and in an abundance of caution, we have decided to temporarily postpone all public events hosted by my campaign. | | | | | | | Friends, After much thought, and in an abundance of caution, we have decided to temporarily postpone all public events hosted by my campaign. This will include all scheduled house parties, fundraisers, and block walks. To all of those who have already RSVP'd for these events, we will let you know once they have been rescheduled. In addition to the campaign, I also recently announced that my pharmacies will be temporarily closing our walk-in business to protect the health of both our employees and customers. We will still be servicing via our drive-thru and curbside service. I think it is important that we take the advice of Governor Abbott and remain particularly vigilant about the potential spread of COVID-19. Please direct yourself to the with any questions about health and prevention. While we are currently postponing public events, that does not mean that our campaign will be taking one minute off! We will be taking full advantage of social media platforms and other technology to to keep our supporters connected and get our message out. If you have not already done so, please follow me on , , and . I also would encourage anyone that would like to get in touch with me and discuss the campaign to message me on social media or email me at mailto:willdouglastx@gmail.com. I hope that you all remain safe, and watch out for friends and neighbors in need. God Bless! \- Will | | "CONTRIBUTE" | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2020 Will Douglas for Texas, All rights reserved.

body:

The COVID–19, also known as coronavirus, has become the singular focus for America and the world.

With that comes the question, are we overreacting? My response to that is to quote from an article by Tom Bossert where he said,

“The paradox of the coronavirus response is that it only works if we take aggressive steps before they seem necessary and, if they work, will not have seemed necessary. Success will look like we overreacted. This is a unique emergency, but the basics still apply.”

CLICK HERE TO READ THE FULL ARTICLE! (http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KTOlkUScQzD_bkOgKuYckesNyD5uaAtbR4cgc_2Q5hHhx59ITGj9l5hZRHhGJ8JsiTj9Dt-2H0a23JvBAPMmGS3a0EQBjj56VlN8yjc0v1uBZVDfhs0HTWTNwHxQiOTS5uLEmYdZxzb49obVhZ4JjJZpKQHlGOwD2qSCgVuCg3n7Rx3gqvC4G1CpbAdLmxgSsm9rMqfzZz6n-SIQlipsn3asBhLgeTRICgNRo58wGSdVbbp0Gk70d76uRyxYYLS4&c=aWnG3E9QBVZy2s7ZEnb8KpE9YUipCH6ZPA71T3U3LGUYbvHWTuApsA==&ch=C20FH06R6DJkuJaycOM5m7FjCPmyEkibevjtW5BNnc-W3e_cgGGDHg==)

We all need to do our part in helping to prevent the spread of the virus. Please keep updated on COVID-19 by visiting the Center for Disease Control’s website: www.cgc.gov (http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KTOlkUScQzD_bkOgKuYckesNyD5uaAtbR4cgc_2Q5hHhx59ITGj9l5hZRHhGJ8Jsk6uKWZilCSx-T58oRgMAj1pWCCIphBJOVkmp2AIBjRwbVNTCtYl8c0mvUeiIl2gUJtsUH7yKT28=&c=aWnG3E9QBVZy2s7ZEnb8KpE9YUipCH6ZPA71T3U3LGUYbvHWTuApsA==&ch=C20FH06R6DJkuJaycOM5m7FjCPmyEkibevjtW5BNnc-W3e_cgGGDHg==) .

Also, keep in mind the following common-sense items to protect yourself and your family.
* Know the symptoms of the virus, which include shortness of breath, cough, and fever. If you think you are experiencing symptoms, please contact your doctor.
* Please wash your hands. Wash your hands for at least 20 seconds. If there is not a sink near you, then use a hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol until you can get to a place with soap and water.
* Stay home if you do not feel well; going out could worsen the spread of the virus and expose at-risk groups like senior citizens.
* Cover your mouth or nose with your elbow or a tissue when you cough or sneeze.

Thank you for actively doing your part to stop the spread of the COVID-19.

Americans are resilient and resourceful, so there is no doubt we will get through this crisis.

Please be safe and take care of yourself and your family.

God Bless,
Dean Browning
Republican for Congress, PA-07

P.S. – While this virus has affected many aspects of our campaign, I am confident we have the tools and grassroots support to overcome this virus and take back the House in November!

subject: Things to do in isolation
body:
We're staying busy giving you ways to help the campaign | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/368c34411e72/things-to-do-in-isolation?e=c56804a14d)
---
| |
---
| |
# I Guess We're Just Staying Home
#
I hope everyone is staying safe in our social isolation. The campaign is staying busy, and while we will not be coming together physically for the foreseeable future, we still have to come together to win in November.

In the interest of giving you things to do as we isolate ourselves, our Volunteer Coordinator is working from home to help you work from home. We need to make calls to the community and to convention delegates. If you're interested in getting your own list to call in the peace of your own home, send Julius a message: julius@iantoddforcongress.com

Just looking for a few minutes to kill or in need of another human voice to hear? We launched our podcast, [On Your 6th](https://IanToddforcongress.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0824156d3c3076099692cab73&id=111061bd92&e=c56804a14d). Take a listen.

Join us today, March 19th, on a [Reddit AMA](https://IanToddforcongress.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0824156d3c3076099692cab73&id=6e13a3797a&e=c56804a14d) (Ask Me Anything) from 3pm to 5pm where I'll be taking your questions.

# COVID-19 Safety Tips

With all the rumors going around, finding the right information to keep you and your loved ones safe may be difficult. The best we can do is arm ourselves with the truth from trusted sources like the CDC.

So if you or someone you know has any of the following symptoms, seek medical assistance by calling your doctor or a local healthcare facility to be advised on what to do. And seeking a second opinion, especially when you feel like your condition is justified, is never wrong.

Per the CDC, symptoms of COVID-19 include the following: Shortness of breath/difficulty breathing, dry cough, and fever. Other likely symptoms include fatigue, headache, or muscle/body aches. Also, note that symptoms may take 2 to 14 days to appear after exposure so even if you are asymptomatic, you have the potential of spreading the illness to others.
I wish you all good health and safety. Take care, everyone

Ian Todd

| Folks, I know we are living through uncertain times. There are now at least 160 positive cases of COVID-19 in my home state of Texas, 3 people have died, and Governor Abbott has activated the Texas National Guard to help handle this developing crisis. I have heard from folks who are worried about passing the virus on to vulnerable friends and family members. I have heard from parents struggling to find childcare, suddenly-unemployed workers wondering how they're going to pay for rent and groceries, and people experiencing homelessness who have nowhere to go. **I want to hear how COVID-19 is affecting you, your family, and your community, and what you need right now from your elected leaders.[So I've opened a community forum where you can send me your thoughts and concerns regarding this public health emergency.](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/vQL9fZTmmfpKN-P97Eb2rodYcom8HEWZt5sIcCt9skw=)** | **[SUBMIT A COMMENT ⇨](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/vQL9fZTmmfpKN-P97Eb2rodYcom8HEWZt5sIcCt9skw=)**
---
I have been working around the clock in Congress to pass bipartisan solutions to slow the spread of this virus and alleviate the burdens it has brought.
Last Wednesday, we passed an $8.3 billion emergency bill to fund vaccine research and support state and local agencies fighting this virus on the front lines. Then, on Friday, we passed a second relief package which includes paid leave, expanded food assistance, extended unemployment insurance, and free coverage for COVID-19 testing.
[**I understand that despite these steps we 've taken, you may still be experiencing anxiety around the spread of COVID-19 and the effect it will have on our communities and our country. I hope you will take a moment to share your concerns with me, so that I can continue to bring your voice to the table as we work to protect the health and safety of all Americans.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/vQL9fZTmmfpKN-P97Eb2rodYcom8HEWZt5sIcCt9skw=)
**[SUBMIT A COMMENT ⇨](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/vQL9fZTmmfpKN-P97Eb2rodYcom8HEWZt5sIcCt9skw=)**
---
Thank you for sharing your voice. I will be in touch soon.
\-- Congressman Al Green
P.S.[**For the most up-to-date information on the COVID-19 pandemic, visit the CDC 's website here.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/FRAeRus__1IlnlWENztyC_7nvvXkfXSb9rA9KsEuQIQ=) In the meantime, you can help flatten the curve by washing your hands frequently and practicing social distancing. I will continue to be in touch as the situation changes.
* * *
| | Congressman Al Green has served the people of Texas' 9th district for 15 years. He is a progressive leader in Washington and was the first Member of Congress to introduce articles of impeachment against President Trump on the House floor.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because we want to keep you fully informed about Al's work for justice, accountability, and progress. If you'd prefer not to hear from us, you can [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/X_s9-iFOXkJ3e9HN8Ohv8EHVNi1SFJ7ul5Vl75BS20k=/-efZCc_1NJeCVCKo_OeFalNrbSs_5kopNgs5o_YEXgc=) here.

name: Jill Vogel
subject: Legislative Update: March
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate 45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100 Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
Since the conclusion of the Senate Session, the Commonwealth of Virginia has been overwhelmed by the impact of the COVID-19 virus
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Senator Jill H. Vogel March 20, 2020 Update Since the conclusion of the Senate Session, the Commonwealth of Virginia has been overwhelmed by the impact of the COVID-19 virus and our office has attempted to respond quickly to anyone with questions or needs. We have also provided updates as necessary to those affected by closures or restrictions issued by the Governor. Our Senate office will remain open to provide support to constituents. Our number one priority is to keep people well. We are also very sensitive to the impact that this is having on families, individuals, businesses, and our local governments. We continue to communicate with the Governor and other state and federal agencies to determine how best to provide support. If you need to reach our office, please email me at [District27@senate.virginia.gov](mailto:District27@senate.virginia.gov). Most questions we receive are about public gatherings and how businesses must function. Governor Northam issued an order directing Virginians to avoid non-essential gatherings of more than 10 people. This restriction does not apply to normal operations of essential services such as transportation services, grocery stores, pharmacies, manufacturing families, or medical facilities. To protect those at higher risk, individuals with chronic health conditions or aged 65 and older have been asked to self-quarantine. name: =?utf-8?Q?Dave=20Lindstrom?=
subject: Support Kansas Businesses
body:
We can all help today | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lindstromforsenate.com/support-kansas-businesses?e=41f8e27552)
---
| |
---
| Dear Friend,

As a business owner and former owner of fast food restaurants, I know firsthand the impact of an economic downturn. However, no previous economic event compares to the challenges faced by business owners and their employees today resulting from the COVID-19 Coronavirus.

COVID-19 has impacted countless Kansas families as layoffs, fewer hours, and fewer job opportunities have resulted. While many Kansans are staying home as a precaution, **where prudently possible we should support our local small businesses and the many thousands of affected Kansas families.**

The federal government is taking steps through the Small Business Administration to ensure credit is available to small businesses. But, these loans will create debt that must be paid back in the future. **__What these businesses really need at this time is customers and cash flow_._**

** There are many local small businesses offering a vast portfolio of services**, from home repair and remodeling to food service, to delivery and transportation, and so many more. None of these services require large “at-risk” gatherings. Be prudent, but don’t be afraid to avail yourself of the many Kansas businesses and the talented Kansans who await your call.

Local restaurants generally remain open, and though some have closed dining rooms, many continue to operate drive-thru, curbside, and takeout service. _Please take advantage of these dining options_.

You can also **buy gift cards** **from your favorite small businesses**. Gift card sales provide immediate cash flow to businesses that can be used to meet payrolls and other obligations, and you can use the gift card in the future.

Small businesses are but one area of our communities impacted by COVID-19. Our neighbors are struggling with various challenges.** ** We can _each_ make a difference, and by coming _together_ we can successfully navigate our present challenges.
---
| |
---

_**Thank you again for standing with me as we take a stand together for our families and our nation!**_ **Dave**

name: Lisa Scheller Campaign
subject: LISA SCHELLER CAMPAIGN ENCOURAGES ALL CITIZENS TO APPLY TO VOTE BY MAIL NOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc
body:
As Americans hunker down in the face of the coronavirus epidemic, Congressional
Candidate Lisa Scheller is calling on citizens to keep our seniors saf

LISA SCHELLER CAMPAIGN ENCOURAGES ALL CITIZENS TO APPLY TO VOTE BY MAIL NOW

FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

Contact:

Dennis Roddy

press@schellerforcongress.com

March 19, 2020

ALLENTOWN, Pa. – As Americans hunker down in the face of the coronavirus
epidemic, Congressional Candidate Lisa Scheller is calling on citizens to keep
our seniors safe by encouraging voters to fill out applications for mail-in
ballots.

Pennsylvania law was recently adjusted to permit “no-excuse” mail-in ballots,
meaning residents don’t have to be absent from their home counties in order to
cast an absentee ballot before election day.

“The health and safety of our citizens is the primary obligation of
government, and I urge voters to take the safest course possible when it comes
to keeping our democracy vibrant,” Scheller said. “A mail-in ballot is an
excellent option, especially for more vulnerable senior citizens, who take
pride in their voting records but are at greatest risk in this pandemic.”

“Given that over 54% of voters in the 7th congressional district are over the
age of 50 and the closure of so many colleges and other institutions, we are
estimating that the majority of ballots cast in the 7th District will be
mail-ins,” said Scheller campaign consultant Ryan Reynolds.

The Scheller campaign has already shifted some of its own resources to
encourage the voting public to file mail-in voting applications in the 7th
District, and has also set up a special webpage for details on how to apply for
a mail-in ballot atsafevote.schellerforcongress.com <>.

“These are not ordinary times, and they call for extra effort and care by
everybody,” Scheller noted. “We need every registered voter to make their
voices heard, whether it’s at the ballot box or the mailbox.”

Unless the Primary is delayed, the deadline to apply for mail-in ballots
remains April 21.

###

name: Team Sri
subject: Please Join Us Tomorrow Evening for a COVID19 Public Health Virtual Town Hall
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
Team,

This is a long email, but I hope you’ll stay with me through to the end.

We’re in a difficult time right now, and it’s likely to be that way for the next few weeks. Sri has been speaking with our statewide leaders and local elected officials to make sure we’re taking the appropriate measures to keep all Texans safe.

It’s important we get accurate and timely information out to residents so that we can slow or stop the spread of this coronavirus also known as COVID-19. I also want to provide you with a few updates on the campaign.

The first and best place to get information is from our State Department of Health and Human Services or the CDC, both of which have been posting several updates a day and have the most accurate information available.

You can help protect yourself by following these recommendations:

Cover coughs or sneezes with your elbow. Do not use your hands!

Wash hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds. Use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer if soap and water are not available.

Clean surfaces frequently, including countertops, light switches, cell phones, remotes, and other frequently touched items.

Contain: if you are sick, stay home until you are feeling better.

As much as Sri and I enjoy attending local meetings and speaking to voters across our district, our campaign will not be holding in-person events or knocking on doors for the remainder of the month. Instead, we are encouraging supporters and volunteers to take action online and do their part to help us #FlattenTheCurve.

We’re launching virtual phone banks, peer-to-peer texting, and some volunteers have asked us to send them postcards to put in the mail while they’re at home.

Although we may be separated physically for the foreseeable future, we will rise above this all together in our goal to bring true representation to TX-22.

Thank you.

Allen Chen

subject: President Trump: Nothing will stand in our way
body:
As America faces an unprecedented global threat from the Coronavirus, President Trump made another important announcement at today’s... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c557a67da9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 19, 2020
---
| | |
# **President Trump: Nothing will stand in our way**

**Medical breakthroughs** are too often held back by outdated, burdensome government rules and regulations. For President Trump, that outcome is unacceptable.

**As America faces** an unprecedented global threat from the Coronavirus, President Trump made another important announcement at today’s White House briefing.

**“Today I want to share** with you exciting progress that the FDA is making with the private sector as we slash red tape like nobody has ever done it before,” he said. “I’ve directed the FDA to eliminate outdated rules and bureaucracy so this work can proceed rapidly, quickly—and, I mean, fast. And we have to remove every barrier.”

🎬 **President Trump:** [“I want to thank all Americans for pulling together”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=360d0c4de2&e=3ee4985eea)

**The FDA is committed** to providing regulatory flexibility and guidance during this crisis, while fulfilling its duty to ensure product safety and effectiveness, FDA Commissioner Dr. Stephen Hahn said during today’s briefing.

**As a result,** obstacles are being cleared for American patients with incredible speed. That includes not only the race to develop a vaccine, but progress in making antiviral therapies widely available. Clinical trials for therapies to fight the Coronavirus are already underway, as well.

**“One thing I know** about this country of ours is that we have unbelievable innovators. We have people who every day, it’s their job to develop treatments for all sorts of diseases,” Dr. Hahn said.

**The** **President** also announced that one existing treatment will soon be available for many patients to try. Chloroquine and its derivative, Hydroxychloroquine, are drugs commonly used to treat malaria. New research shows they may hold unique promise as potentially effective treatments for Coronavirus, too.

**While people** of all backgrounds unite against this invisible enemy, President Trump wants every American to know that we face this historic challenge together.

**Last night,** the President [signed the Family First bill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=57cea5b205&e=3ee4985eea), which provides crucial support for our workers, small businesses, and families in the coming days. Paid sick leave and paid medical leave will be there for those affected by the virus. And more help is on the way.

**Tens of thousands of tests** are being performed each day and are available in all 50 states, Vice President Mike Pence announced. Access to medical equipment is being rapidly expanded to all who need it, including businesses, hospitals, and state and local leaders.

**“We’ve literally identified** tens of thousands of ventilators that can be converted to treat patients. And we remain increasingly confident that we will have the ventilators that we need as the Coronavirus makes its way across America,” the Vice President said.

**🎬** [Once America conquers the virus, our economy will come roaring back!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=63cdf93453&e=3ee4985eea)

**WATCH:** [**President and Vice President teleconference with governors at FEMA HQ**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1775833637&e=3ee4985eea "https://twitter.com/WhiteHouse/status/1240720886203846663")
---
|
---
|
## 🎥 **First Lady: We are all in this together**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7651015421&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Americans****are making** important and difficult lifestyle changes to protect themselves and their families. While these adjustments can be disruptive, First Lady Melania Trump offers an encouraging reminder: “This is not how we will live forever.”

**“Our children** will return to school, people will return to work,” she says. “We will gather at the places of worship, concerts, and sporting events again.”

**Until then,** remember two important things: First, stay connected with loved ones using technology as much as possible. Second, keep following all CDC public health guidelines, which can be found along with regular updates at [www.coronavirus.gov](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d13ad68cde&e=3ee4985eea).

**“Stay safe,** and remember, while many of us are apart, we are all in this together.”

**🎬****Dr. Anthony Fauci:** [What to do if you feel sick](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0d964e3804&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

name: MJ Hegar
subject: Update: Our COVID-19 Mission
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
I want to give you another update on the mission at hand: protecting folks from the spread of this pandemic and the economic fallout.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890210/7f51f7bc/4c1a2c81/4ef1e40c/463639383/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Team,

First and foremost, I want to start by thanking the workers on the frontlines of this pandemic. Those keeping health care facilities running, keeping food available in stores and for delivery, keeping our streets and buildings clean and sanitized, responding to emergencies, and beyond are helping keep us safe and healthy right now. If that's you: **_thank you._**

Now, I want to give you another update on the mission at hand: protecting folks from the spread of this pandemic and the economic fallout.

**What we know:**
* Yesterday, the Senate passed a COVID-19 relief bill to ensure free testing, take steps to enact emergency paid sick leave, and expand food assistance and unemployment insurance during this crisis. The reality is this was several days overdue, but I am glad the Senate finally took action.

* Next, Congress will consider further legislation to mitigate the economic impact on American families and businesses. **Americans need an economic stimulus package to help them get through COVID-19 -- ASAP. Solutions should focus on making workers and small businesses whole and NOT be a giveaway to huge corporations and their CEOs.**

**What we 're doing as a campaign:**
* My campaign is doing our part to flatten the curve by working remotely and suspending in-person events. We're planning next steps so we can keep campaigning virtually.

* We will continue to fundraise so we can get our message out to Texans through phone calls, text messages, and ads because one thing has become starkly clear: we need a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[If you'd like to pitch in, you can do so here >>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890210/7f51f7bc/4c1a2c81/4ef1e403/463639383/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTgifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-donation-tx?refcode=email_20200319.2nd__1098__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

* Other campaigns can rely on the special interests to fill the gap, but I'm not taking corporate PAC money, so if we're going to win this election we need to keep hitting our fundraising goals.

* However, I know that this is not an easy time for so many. **[If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, please let us know here -- I'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890210/7f51f7bc/4c1a2c81/4ef1e402/463639383/VEsF/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**

**What we need:**
* In the coming days, you'll start to hear scapegoating from public officials -- in fact, it's already started. We can't fall into the trap of division -- unity is critical right now. I'm urging all of our elected officials to put people over politics and focus on the mission at hand: getting through this with as little harm to the American people as possible.

* Elected officials should work quickly to pass and implement immediate solutions to increase health care capacity to take care of the sick, and to ensure that our front-line health care workers have every health and safety protection we can provide -- especially adequate testing and personal protective equipment.

* This should be a major wake-up call for our representatives. Texas has the highest uninsured rate in the nation, and it puts folks here at greater risk during this pandemic. **We need a public health insurance option to ensure all Americans can receive health care without facing impossible financial choices.**

We're all in this together, and we all need to do our part. If we do, we'll come out on the other side of this with less harm done and a model of solidarity and teamwork for generations to come.

We know what our mission is, so let's get it done.

\-- MJ

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Gov. Holcomb: Helping Hoosiers Impacted
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Sometimes the best defense is a good offense, and we have to go on offense to
slow the spread.

<>
Friend,

This afternoon, Governor Holcomb said it best: “Sometimes the best defense is
a good offense, and we have to go on offense to slow the spread.”

And Indiana has all its players on the court working together for Hoosiers –
taking unprecedented actions to protect Hoosiers and extend relief to business
and individuals impacted by the changes we’re seeing today in response to the
coronavirus.

Here’s just some of the BIG actions and executive orders Governor Holcomb
signed today –

State of Emergency

* Indiana’s state of emergency will be extended an additional 30 days when it
expires on April 5

K-12 Schools

* All of Indiana’s K-12 public schools will remain closed through May 1, and
non-public schools are also ordered closed. This date could be extended if the
circumstances warrant.
* All state-mandated assessments will be canceled for the current academic
year.
* The governor has contacted U.S. Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos to share
the state’s plan and also has asked the Superintendent of Public Instruction
Dr. Jennifer McCormick to pursue any federal waivers needed to cancel the
requirements for accountability, chronic absenteeism and state-mandated
assessments.

Indiana’s Economy

* The deadline to pay your state income taxes will be extended from April 15
to July 15 to align with new federal dates.
* Penalties will be waived for 60 days for property taxes paid after May 11.
The state will work with counties that may experience cash flow stress because
of the delay.
* Indiana will not immediately move forward with using $300 million in
reserves to pay for several capital projects approved in the just-concluded
legislative session and instead maintain flexibility to utilize the funds as
needed for relief efforts and to maintain current services.
* Providers of essential utility services such as gas and electric,
broadband, telecom, water and wastewater services are prohibited from
discontinuing service to any customer during the public health emergency.
* The state’s application to the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) for
an Economic Injury Disaster Loan declaration, per Governor Holcomb’s request,
has been approved. This program provides targeted, low-interest loans of up to
$2 million to help small businesses and nonprofits overcome the temporary loss
of revenue as a result of coronavirus. See more atSBA.gov/Disaster <>.

Unemployment Insurance Benefits

* The state will interpret Indiana’s unemployment laws to the broadest extent
possible to cover Hoosiers who are out of work because of COVID-19.
* Benefits will be paid to individuals who file their initial unemployment
claims late.
* The Department of Workforce Development will allow individuals to continue
to accrue unemployment eligibility if they take work leave because of COVID-19.
* DWD will seek federal authorization to provide unemployment benefits for
those who are not otherwise eligible for unemployment, such individuals who
have recently started a job.
* For employers, DWD will not assess certain experience rate penalties
because of employees who receive unemployment benefits because of COVID-19.

Housing

* No residential eviction proceedings or foreclosure actions may be initiated
during the public health emergency. This does not relieve the individual of
obligations to pay rent or mortgage payments.
* All public housing authorities are requested to extend deadlines for
housing assistance recipients and required documentation to show eligibility
for housing programs.

Social Services

* Participants in the Healthy Indiana Plan (HIP) and the Children’s Health
Insurance Program are not required to make premium payments.
* Job search requirements are waived for those applying for Temporary
Assistance to Needy Families (TANF) benefits.
* The Family and Social Services Administration will seek a federal waiver to
extend renewals for existing Medicaid and HIP recipients.
* Telehealth services for mental health, substance use disorder and
prescribing for Medicaid covered services will be expanded.

Insurance

* The commissioner of the Department of Insurance will request that insurers
institute a 60-day moratorium on policy cancellations for non-payment of
premiums. This does not suspend a policyholder’s obligation to make payments.
* The commissioner will ask health insurers to cover COVID-19 testing without
requiring prior authorization, and also request that health insurers not
increase prices or coverage costs that involve medical care for COVID-19.

Bureau of Motor Vehicles

* To limit the number of in-branch transactions, late fees will be waived for
several driver’s licenses and identification card renewals, vehicle
registrations, titles, and certain other transactions.
* Other operational changes in branches are being instituted to provide for
the safety of employees and customers in branches, such as spacing between
terminals and limiting the number of customers in the lobby.

Veterans

* Requirements have been relaxed for veterans to qualify for awards from the
Military Family Relief Fund. Awards in excess of $2,500 may be approved by the
IDVA director during the public health emergency.

Health and Professional Licensing

* Mental health professionals are permitted to practice via telemedicine.
* Advance Practice Registered Nurses are allowed to provide services in
multiple locations.
* The state health commissioner may waive requirements of the nursing home
certificate of need statute to respond to COVID-19 issues for long-term care
facilities.

Here with the Holcomb Crew, we join Governor Holcomb in thanking each of you
for your work to help keep our fellow Hoosiers safe during this difficult time.
We’re here for you – and we’re all in this together.

"Here's to our persistence. Here is to our personal responsibility. And here's
to our confidence to finish this marathon." – Gov. Holcomb

Thanks,
-Your Holcomb Crew

46204 | (317) 569-0709

name: Reverend Warnock
subject: John Lewis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
fought for our right to vote - and we have a moral obligation to use it.
| | |
---
Friend,

As the senior pastor of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s church, I proudly stand on the shoulders of a long line of freedom fighters. **Congressman John Lewis, my fellow Georgian, is one of them. **

Generations of moral and courageous leaders like him have fought and died for our right to vote - because making our voices heard at the ballot box is the most powerful tool we have as Americans.
**John Lewis has been at the forefront of every major civil rights fight of the last half-century - and he continues to lead in protecting voting rights for all. [In honor of his trailblazing legacy, will you add your name alongside mine to pledge to vote this November, in what will be one of the most important elections of our lifetimes?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6cb/288179be/a86c5b6/348ee8c0/2113538128/VEsH/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://sign.actiontaker.org/Lewis-Pledge-Warnock-AB-nd")**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6cb/288179be/a86c5b6/348ee8c0/2113538128/VEsE/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://sign.actiontaker.org/Lewis-Pledge-Warnock-AB-nd")
---
We are in a fight for the soul of our nation's democracy, friend.

And voting - raising our collective voices to create lasting change - has never been more important. With everything from health care to criminal justice reform on the ballot this year, we simply cannot afford to stay silent.
**John Lewis fought for our right to vote - and we have a moral obligation to use it. That's why I need 5,493 more people to add their names by midnight tonight, and I'm counting on you to be one of them: [Stand with Congressman John Lewis, and pledge to vote in November!](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6cb/288179be/a86c5b6/348ee8c0/2113538128/VEsF/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://sign.actiontaker.org/Lewis-Pledge-Warnock-AB-nd")**
| | [PLEDGE TO VOTE](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6cb/288179be/a86c5b6/348ee8c0/2113538128/VEsC/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://sign.actiontaker.org/Lewis-Pledge-Warnock-AB-nd")
---
Thank you for joining me in the fight for justice.

- Reverend Warnock

subject: Gov. Holcomb: Here's to Finishing This Marathon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
Today Gov. Holcomb addressed all Hoosiers when he said he hears you. He wants to know he's listening -- and today he took some big actions to help... |
---
| |
---
| **Gov. Holcomb: Here's to Finishing This Marathon**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3453827e5c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| Today, Gov. Holcomb addressed all Hoosiers when he said he hears you. He wants you to know he's listening -- and today he took some big actions to help our fellow Hoosiers across the state impacted by challenges related to the coronavirus outbreak.
[\--> Watch What He Said Here <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1aa804fe0f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
[\--> Read a List of Today's Actions Here <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4e12cc7e02&e=1acd9bb3d6)
_**"I talk to Hoosiers border to border, from different walks of life, and this has rocked their world, understandably. It has changed everything. And I want them to know, that that's why I want to be in constant communication. The Lt. Governor and I want to know what the individual challenge is...what the barrier is, whether it's unemployment or questions about where my kid's going to eat -- we want to help them solve that problem. And with millions of Hoosiers in the same boat, we got to look out for each other."**_ _**-Governor Eric Holcomb**_
Some of those actions include:
* Extending Indiana's state of emergency by another 30 days when it expires on April 5
* All of Indiana’s K-12 public schools will remain closed through May 1, and non-public schools are also ordered closed.
* The deadline to pay your state income taxes will be extended from April 15 to July 15 to align with new federal dates, and penalties will be waived for 60 days for property taxes paid after May 11.
* Providers of essential utilities are prohibited from discontinuing services during the public health emergency.
* Small businesses can apply for disaster loans through the Small Business Administration to overcome temporary revenue losses
* The state will interpret Indiana’s unemployment laws to the broadest extent possible to cover Hoosiers who are out of work because of COVID-19.
* No residential eviction proceedings or foreclosure actions may be initiated during the public health emergency.
* The commissioner of the Department of Insurance will request that insurers institute a 60-day moratorium on policy cancellations for non-payment of premiums.
* To limit the number of in-branch transactions, late fees will be waived for several driver’s licenses and identification card renewals, vehicle registrations, titles, and certain other transactions.
* Mental health professionals are permitted to practice via telemedicine.
* [View the full list of actions here.](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a36f6bd76a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
